title: Lisocabtagene maraleucel for patients with relapsed or refractory large B-cell lymphomas (TRANSCEND NHL 001): a multicentre seamless design study
design: Multicentre, multicohort, open-label, seamless-design phase 1 study of liso-cel (CD19 CAR T) at 14 US cancer centres; NCT02631044. Three target dose levels; efficacy-evaluable set n=256, safety set n=269, ITT (leukapheresed) n=344.
population: Adults with relapsed/refractory LBCL after >=2 prior lines (including anti-CD20 + anthracycline). Histologies: DLBCL NOS, DLBCL transformed from indolent lymphoma, high-grade B-cell lymphoma (double/triple-hit), PMBCL, FL grade 3B. Median 3 prior lines (range 1-8); 67% chemorefractory; 42% aged >=65; heavily pretreated.
line: Third-line or later (3L+); >=2 prior systemic therapies required.
key_eligibility: Secondary CNS lymphoma explicitly ALLOWED/eligible (7 [3%] had secondary CNS involvement) - grounds permissive CNS rule for liso-cel 3L+
Moderate comorbidities permitted: creatinine clearance >30 to <60 mL/min and LVEF >=40% to <50%; no minimum absolute lymphocyte count
Bridging therapy allowed during manufacturing but required reconfirmation of PET-positive disease before lymphodepletion
Broader eligibility than ZUMA-1/JULIET (included older, comorbid, CNS-involved patients)
outcomes: Efficacy-evaluable set (n=256): ORR 73% (95% CI 66.8-78.0), CR 53% (46.8-59.4). Median DoR not reached; median PFS 6.8 months; median OS 21.1 months; 12-month OS 58%. Safety (n=269): CRS any grade 42% (grade >=3 only 2% [6 patients]); neurological events any grade 30% (grade >=3 10%). Grade >=3 neutropenia 60%, anaemia 37%, thrombocytopenia 27%. Low rate of severe CRS/neurotoxicity is a signature feature.
grounds_rubric_claims: liso-cel is an approved 3L+ CD19 CAR T option for R/R LBCL with ORR ~73%/CR ~53%
liso-cel permits secondary CNS lymphoma involvement (unlike some CAR products/trials) - CNS involvement is not an absolute exclusion
liso-cel has comparatively low grade >=3 CRS (2%) and neurotoxicity (10%), supporting use in higher-risk/comorbid or older patients
Broad histologic eligibility including transformed indolent lymphoma, double/triple-hit HGBCL, PMBCL, FL3B

verbatim_quotes.quote: 181 (67%) had chemotherapy-refractory disease, and seven (3%) had secondary CNS involvement.
verbatim_quotes.supports: TRANSCEND enrolled and permitted patients with secondary CNS lymphoma (3% of cohort), grounding a permissive-CNS rubric rule for liso-cel

verbatim_quotes.quote: an objective response was achieved by 186 (73%, 95% CI 66.8-78.0) patients and a complete response by 136 (53%, 46.8-59.4).
verbatim_quotes.supports: Headline efficacy for liso-cel 3L+: ORR 73%, CR 53%

verbatim_quotes.quote: grade 3 or worse cytokine release syndrome and neurological events occurred in six (2%) and 27 (10%) patients, respectively.
verbatim_quotes.supports: Low incidence of severe CRS and neurological events, a defining safety advantage of liso-cel
